ServiceProvider.title: ServiceProvider
ServiceProvider.description: Represents an independent service provider registered with the National Cleaning Company, managing their profile, schedule, and financial information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceProvider entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the service provider.

lastName.description: The last name of the service provider.

email.description: The email address of the service provider, used for communication.

phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number of the service provider.

addressStreet.description: The street address of the service provider.

addressCity.description: The city part of the service provider's address.

addressState.description: The state or province part of the service provider's address.

addressZip.description: The postal or ZIP code part of the service provider's address.

companyName.description: The name of the service provider's independent company, if applicable.

serviceAreaZipCodes.description: A list of ZIP codes or postal codes defining the service provider's operational area.

status.description: The current status of the service provider (e.g., 'pending_approval', 'active', 'inactive').

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the service provider registered.

bio.description: A brief biography or description of the service provider's experience and services.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the service provider's profile picture.

taxIdNumber.description: The tax identification number for accounting and payment purposes.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the service provider from completed jobs.

adminNotes.description: Internal notes added by administrators about the service provider.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer who uses the National Cleaning Company's services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the client.

lastName.description: The last name of the client.

email.description: The email address of the client, used for communication.

phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number of the client.

addressStreet.description: The street address where services are typically rendered for the client.

addressCity.description: The city part of the client's service address.

addressState.description: The state or province part of the client's service address.

addressZip.description: The postal or ZIP code part of the client's service address.

companyName.description: The name of the client's company, if they are a business client.

notes.description: Internal notes added by administrators or service providers about the client.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the client registered with the company.

Administrator.title: Administrator
Administrator.description: Represents an internal administrator of the National Cleaning Company who manages operations, clients, and service providers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Administrator entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the administrator.

lastName.description: The last name of the administrator.

email.description: The email address of the administrator.

phoneNumber.description: The primary phone number of the administrator.

role.description: The specific role or department of the administrator (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'Operations', 'Finance', 'Support').

hireDate.description: The date when the administrator was hired.

ServiceJob.title: ServiceJob
ServiceJob.description: Represents a single cleaning service job assigned to a service provider for a client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceJob entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client requesting the service. (Relationship: Client 1:N ServiceJob)

serviceProviderId.description: Reference to the ServiceProvider assigned to this job. (Relationship: ServiceProvider 1:N ServiceJob)

serviceType.description: The type of cleaning service requested (e.g., 'Standard Residential Clean', 'Deep Commercial Clean').

scheduledStartTime.description: The planned start date and time for the service job.

scheduledEndTime.description: The planned end date and time for the service job.

actualStartTime.description: The actual start date and time when the service provider began the job.

actualEndTime.description: The actual end date and time when the service provider completed the job.

status.description: The current status of the service job (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled', 'Rescheduled').

specialInstructions.description: Any specific instructions or requests from the client for this job.

providerNotes.description: Notes added by the service provider regarding the job.

clientRating.description: The rating (e.g., 1-5 stars) given by the client for this specific job.

clientFeedback.description: Textual feedback provided by the client after job completion.

price.description: The total monetary price for this service job.

paymentStatus.description: The payment status of the job (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Refunded').

creationDate.description: The date and time when the service job was created.

ScheduleEntry.title: ScheduleEntry
ScheduleEntry.description: Represents an entry in a service provider's schedule, indicating availability, a specific job, or blocked time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ScheduleEntry entity.

serviceProviderId.description: Reference to the ServiceProvider whose schedule this entry belongs to. (Relationship: ServiceProvider 1:N ScheduleEntry)

startTime.description: The start date and time of the schedule entry.

endTime.description: The end date and time of the schedule entry.

serviceJobId.description: Optional reference to a ServiceJob if this schedule entry corresponds to a job. (Relationship: ServiceJob 0:1 ScheduleEntry)

description.description: A brief description of the schedule entry, especially for non-job related times (e.g., 'Lunch Break').

status.description: The status of the schedule entry (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'Tentative', 'Cancelled').

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents a bill issued to a client for services rendered, detailing amounts due and payment status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client to whom this invoice is issued. (Relationship: Client 1:N Invoice)

issueDate.description: The date and time when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is expected.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount due on this invoice.

status.description: The current payment status of the invoice (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Partially Paid', 'Voided').

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the invoice was fully paid.

serviceJobIds.description: References to ServiceJob entities included in this invoice. (Relationship: ServiceJob N:N Invoice)

notes.description: Any additional notes or internal comments related to the invoice.

PayoutTransaction.title: PayoutTransaction
PayoutTransaction.description: Represents a financial transaction from the company to a service provider, such as payment for services or bonuses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PayoutTransaction entity.

serviceProviderId.description: Reference to the ServiceProvider receiving this payout. (Relationship: ServiceProvider 1:N PayoutTransaction)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

status.description: The current status of the payout (e.g., 'Pending', 'Paid', 'Failed').

description.description: A description of the payout transaction (e.g., 'Weekly payout for jobs completed').

relatedServiceJobIds.description: Optional references to ServiceJob entities that this payout covers. (Relationship: ServiceJob N:N PayoutTransaction)

relatedInvoiceId.description: Optional reference to an Invoice if this payout is directly linked to client payment for that invoice. (Relationship: Invoice 0:1 PayoutTransaction)

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a communication thread between service providers, clients, and/or administrators within the internal messaging system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

title.description: A user-friendly title or subject for the conversation.

participantIds.description: An array of unique identifiers for all participants in this conversation. These IDs can refer to ServiceProvider, Client, or Administrator entities. (Relationship: ServiceProvider N:N Conversation, Client N:N Conversation, Administrator N:N Conversation)

lastMessageTimestamp.description: The date and time of the most recent message in the conversation.

status.description: The current status of the conversation (e.g., 'Active', 'Archived', 'Resolved').

relatedServiceJobId.description: Optional reference to a ServiceJob if the conversation is directly related to a specific job. (Relationship: ServiceJob 0:1 Conversation)

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message sent within a conversation in the internal messaging system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: The ID of the user who sent the message. This can be a ServiceProvider, Client, or Administrator ID.

senderType.description: The type of entity that sent the message (e.g., 'ServiceProvider', 'Client', 'Administrator'). Used in conjunction with senderId for polymorphic referencing.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

content.description: The textual content of the message.

isReadByRecipientIds.description: An array of IDs of the participants in the conversation who have read this message. (Relationship: ServiceProvider N:N Message, Client N:N Message, Administrator N:N Message)

definition.description: Stores the profile information for an independent service provider. The document ID (`serviceProviderId`) directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the service provider. This collection defines the 'Service Provider' role for DBAC. Only the service provider themselves can read/write their own profile, and administrators can manage all profiles.

params.name: serviceProviderId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the service provider, acting as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores the profile information for a client. The document ID (`clientId`) directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the client. This collection defines the 'Client' role for DBAC. Only the client themselves can read/write their own profile, and administrators can manage all profiles.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the client, acting as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores the profile information for an internal administrator. The document ID (`administratorId`) directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator. This collection defines the 'Administrator' role for DBAC ('Existence over Content'). Only administrators can read/write their own profiles, and potentially super-admins manage all profiles.

params.name: administratorId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator, acting as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores details for each cleaning service job. Documents include denormalized `clientId` and `serviceProviderId` fields for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule checks (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.clientId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.serviceProviderId`) without `get()` calls. This enables secure queries for clients and service providers to list their relevant jobs.

params.name: serviceJobId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service job.

definition.description: Stores individual schedule entries (jobs, availability, blocked time) for a specific service provider. Ownership is established by the path segment `serviceProviderId`, which directly maps to the `request.auth.uid` for authorization independence. This ensures that only the relevant service provider or an administrator can access these entries.

params.name: serviceProviderId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the service provider whose schedule this entry belongs to.

params.name: scheduleEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the schedule entry.

definition.description: Stores invoices issued to a specific client. Ownership is established by the path segment `clientId`, which directly maps to the `request.auth.uid` for authorization independence. Only the relevant client or an administrator can access their invoices.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the client to whom this invoice is issued.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice.

definition.description: Stores payout transactions for a specific service provider. Ownership is established by the path segment `serviceProviderId`, which directly maps to the `request.auth.uid` for authorization independence. Only the relevant service provider or an administrator can access their payout records.

params.name: serviceProviderId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the service provider receiving this payout.

params.name: payoutTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payout transaction.

definition.description: Stores communication threads involving multiple participants (Service Providers, Clients, Administrators). Documents include a denormalized `participantIds` array, which contains the UIDs of all authorized users. This enables authorization independence, allowing direct security rule checks (e.g., `request.auth.uid in resource.data.participantIds`) without `get()` calls.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the conversation.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a conversation. Documents include the denormalized `participantIds` array (copied from the parent `Conversation`) for authorization independence. This allows direct security rule checks for message access without requiring a `get()` call to the parent `Conversation` document.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability in line with the core design principles. The primary goal is to ensure Authorization Independence, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which simplifies rule logic and supports atomic operations like transactions and batch writes. This is achieved through strategic denormalization and structural segregation.

1.  **Authorization Independence & Denormalization:**
    *   **User Roles:** Separate top-level collections (`/serviceProviders`, `/clients`, `/administrators`) are used to segregate different user types. The document ID for each user is their Firebase `request.auth.uid`. The mere existence of a document in these collections acts as a direct, database-driven access control (DBAC) mechanism, defining their primary role without custom claims.
    *   **Shared Entities (`ServiceJob`, `Conversation`, `Message`):** For entities accessed by multiple user types (e.g., `ServiceJob` by `Client` and `ServiceProvider`), critical authorization fields are denormalized directly into the document. `ServiceJob` documents will explicitly contain `clientId` and `serviceProviderId` fields. `Conversation` documents will contain a `participantIds` array. `Message` documents, being a subcollection of `Conversation`, will also denormalize the `participantIds` from their parent `Conversation` to allow atomic and independent authorization checks. This ensures that a user's access can be determined by directly comparing `request.auth.uid` against these fields within the `resource.data` of the document itself, without needing to `get()` a parent or related document.
    *   **User-Owned Subcollections:** For data intrinsically owned by a single user (e.g., `ScheduleEntry` by `ServiceProvider`, `Invoice` by `Client`, `PayoutTransaction` by `ServiceProvider`), hierarchical paths are used (e.g., `/serviceProviders/{serviceProviderId}/scheduleEntries`). Here, `serviceProviderId` or `clientId` in the path segment directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, achieving authorization independence naturally.

2.  **QAPs (Query, Access, Performance, Security) for Secure `list` Operations:**
    *   **Path-Based Ownership:** For subcollections like `/serviceProviders/{serviceProviderId}/scheduleEntries` or `/clients/{clientId}/invoices`, `list` operations are inherently secure. Security rules can enforce that `serviceProviderId` or `clientId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`, ensuring users only list their own data.
    *   **Denormalized Authorization Fields:** For collections like `/serviceJobs` and `/conversations`, the denormalized `clientId`, `serviceProviderId`, and `participantIds` fields enable secure `list` queries. Rules can ensure that a `list` operation is only allowed if a filter (`where` clause) includes `clientId == request.auth.uid` (for clients), `serviceProviderId == request.auth.uid` (for service providers), or `request.auth.uid in participantIds` (for conversations). Administrators, whose `uid` exists in `/administrators`, would have broader `list` permissions across all collections.
    *   **Structural Segregation:** Separating entities with different security postures into distinct collections (e.g., all `ServiceProviders` in one collection, all `Clients` in another) significantly simplifies security rules, as rules can be applied uniformly to an entire collection rather than needing complex conditional logic within a single mixed collection.

This design leverages Firebase Authentication for user identity (email/password, Google OAuth) and then uses the `request.auth.uid` to map directly to dedicated user profile documents and to enforce granular access across all data entities, ensuring a robust, easily debuggable, and scalable security model.
